title: The Canterbury Tales (Corpus MS 198/Furnivall representation)
summary: The tracked XML is a copy of a 2006 U-M uncorrected OCR/Level-1 encoding of Furnivall's seven-part 1868-1884 Corpus-MS edition. Furnivall transcribed Corpus Christi College, Oxford, MS 198 from A73 through an imperfect Parson endpoint, while explicitly supplying and appending passages from Arch. Selden B.14, Laud 600, Christ Church 152, Hatton donat.1, Harley 1758, Harley 1753, Royal 17 D XV, Harley 7333, Royal 19 C VII, and an unresolved Haistwell manuscript. The graph keeps the abstract work, physical witnesses, edition, scans, facsimiles, encodings, catalogs, and rights layers distinct.
review_notes: Royal MS 19 C VII contributes an incidental Middle French source quotation at Melibee paragraph 2765, not an English target-work passage; its complete local facsimile therefore has target_work_presence absent.
Complete provider presentations have whole-to-whole facsimile relations; selected-slide Selden and Hatton presentations have leaf-level scoped relations.
Shared entity IDs match adjacent Canterbury Tales records for Arch. Selden B.14, Christ Church MS 152, Harley MS 1758, Royal MS 17 D XV, and Royal MS 19 C VII.

entities.label: General Corpus repository XML agz8235
entities.description: Tracked repository serialization copied from the U-M CME encoding; this is not the abstract work, manuscript, edition, or U-M item.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic text AGZ8235.0001.001
entities.description: Uncorrected OCR and Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding created in 2006 from Furnivall's printed Corpus-MS edition and linked to 756 page images.

entities.label: U-M CME page-image presentation for AGZ8235.0001.001
entities.description: The 756 unique 600-dpi TIFF G4 page-image references linked from the electronic text, reproducing the scanned printed carrier.
notes: No documented public bulk-download endpoint for the exact image set was verified.

entities.label: Furnivall's Corpus MS edition of The Canterbury Tales, 1868-1884
alternate_labels: The Corpus MS (Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford) of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
entities.description: Seven-part Chaucer Society one-manuscript edition, principally transcribing Corpus Christi College, Oxford, MS 198 while explicitly supplying lacunae and appendices from named manuscripts.

bibliographic.citation: Chaucer, Geoffrey. The Corpus MS (Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford) of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales. Edited by Frederick J. Furnivall. London: published for the Chaucer Society by N. Trübner & Co., 1868-1884.
bibliographic.title: The Corpus MS (Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford) of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Chaucer Society
bibliographic.pages: viii, [2], 154 p., 1 leaf, 155-700 [i.e. 705], 8* p., 2 leaves, 14 surviving plates

notes: The audited carrier lacks nine of the twenty-three plates indicated in the contents and a nine-plate Gg.4.27 suite, which the source note says may never have been issued.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Oxford, MS 198
entities.description: Middle English Canterbury Tales manuscript produced in London by the catalog's Scribe D; imperfect, beginning at General Prologue A73 and ending in the Parson's Tale after I290.

holding.notes: Digital Bodleian metadata gives 267 leaves and labels the digitized text from fol. 2r through fol. 266v; a contents note elsewhere says ff. 1-266v.

physical_description.extent: 267 leaves; ff. 2-266 including 64a and 115a in the digitized labeling
notes: Single expert hand identified by the catalog as Scribe D in anglicana formata.
Nineteenth-century polished calf binding, rebacked in 1987.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete presentation of Corpus Christi College MS 198
notes: The public service returned approximately 1000-pixel-high derivatives for requests using the 1600, profile.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan corpusmscorpusch00chaurich
entities.description: Digitization of an imperfect printed carrier of Furnivall's Corpus-MS edition.
entities.reproduction_format: 740-page PDF from the Internet Archive item
notes: Complete for the scanned carrier but partial relative to the edition's announced visual apparatus.

entities.label: Internet Archive OCR for corpusmscorpusch00chaurich
notes: Used as a discovery aid; readings and boundaries were checked against the PDF and repository XML.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Arch. Selden B.14
entities.description: Middle English Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall for extensive bracketed supplies and appendix texts.

notes: Digital Bodleian describes a Middle English manuscript made in England.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected-slide presentation of Arch. Selden B.14
notes: A complete download of the provider's eleven selected slides, not a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 600
entities.description: Canterbury Tales manuscript used for the large Melibee supply and most of the Squire-Franklin link.

entities.label: Christ Church, Oxford, MS 152
entities.description: Fifteenth-century manuscript containing a Canterbury Tales sequence and the sixteen-line Nun's Priest-Manciple end-link printed by Furnivall.

notes: The provider describes a complete Canterbury Tales in an unusual order together with Lydgate poems.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete presentation of Christ Church MS 152
Bodleian Library, MS Hatton donat. 1
entities.description: Middle English Canterbury Tales manuscript used for the final portion of the Retraction; Furnivall's short label is resolved here to the Chaucer manuscript Hatton donat. 1, not the unrelated Hatton 1 missal.

notes: Digital Bodleian describes East Midland language with some Northern forms.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected-slide presentation of Hatton donat. 1
notes: The selected slides do not include target folio 275b or Furnivall's Retraction supply.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1758
entities.description: Canterbury Tales manuscript used for one Summoner passage and three Monk passages.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1753
entities.description: Canterbury Tales manuscript used for one Monk line and kept distinct from Harley MS 1758.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 7333
entities.description: Canterbury Tales manuscript used for a five-line Nun's Priest extract.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 17 D XV
entities.description: Canterbury Tales manuscript used for Pardoner and Nun's Priest sequence insertions.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 19 C VII
entities.description: French manuscript cited for the source-language phrase in Melibee paragraph 2765 at f. 142v; not an English Canterbury Tales base witness.

entities.label: British Library IIIF presentation of Royal MS 19 C VII
notes: The resolver returned full service derivatives despite a requested 1600, profile; exact requested and returned URLs are preserved in the bundle inventory.

entities.label: Unresolved 'Haistwell MS'
entities.description: Historical source label attached to Squire-Franklin link F679-680; present identity and shelfmark unresolved.
notes: Not conflated with Hatton donat. 1 or any other witness.

entities.label: GND authority record 117540579 for Frederick James Furnivall
Thomas Tyrwhitt, The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer (1775-1778)
entities.description: Five-volume edition consulted by Furnivall for paragraph division in Melibee and the Parson's Tale; it is supporting editorial apparatus, not a manuscript source for the Corpus text.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 739
entities.description: Comparison witness cited only for omission of Wife of Bath's Preamble D716-720; it does not supply the edition's base text.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Barlow 20
entities.description: Comparison witness cited only for omission of Wife of Bath's Preamble D605-608; it does not supply the edition's base text.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 18 C II
entities.description: Comparison witness cited because Nun's Priest lines B4112 and B4114 are absent; it does not supply those lines in this edition.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 1685
entities.description: Comparison witness cited for absence of Nun's Priest B4233-4237 at f. 217v.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 1686
entities.description: Comparison witness identified by Furnivall as Sloane 1686, f. 285, for absence of Nun's Priest B4233-4237; identity with the current shelfmark remains to be checked against images.

entities.label: Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9 (Ellesmere Chaucer)
entities.description: Comparison witness for two Franklin passages and visual source for the edition's twenty-three teller portrait plates; it is not the edition's base manuscript.

entities.label: Huntington Ellesmere Chaucer IIIF presentation
entities.description: Official Huntington digital presentation used to verify the twenty-three portrait loci and the two Franklin comparison passages.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Gg.4.27
entities.description: Visual source named for six teller figures and six allegorical figures announced in the edition; the checked carrier's records leave their actual issuance unresolved.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library MS Gg.4.27.1 IIIF presentation
entities.description: Official 1,144-canvas presentation used to verify the edition's announced visual-source loci.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian two-canvas selected presentation of MS Barlow 20
entities.description: Provider-published selected images of ff. 5r and 44r; physically partial and not containing the D605-608 comparison locus.

subject.description: Exact bracketed supplies, appendices, and links explicitly attributed to Arch. Selden B.14
locators: General Prologue A1-72; single bracketed readings A119 [nd], A686 [lay], and A713 [right]
Wife's Preamble D10; D146 after 'Oure lord' through D217; D575-584; D609-612; D619-626; D717-720
Clerk head-link E33-56; Clerk tale E57-107; E117, E143, E173; Clerk envoy E1190-1212
Clerk-Merchant link E1213-1244; Merchant E1245-1292, E1305-1306, E1308, E1646-1647, E1927-1928, appendix E2319-2418, Merchant-Squire link E2419-2440; eight unnumbered Squire head-link lines
Blank-Parson link I62-74; Parson opening [75] through [104] phrase 'of which synne þe fame is openly'; continuation within [290] after 'he shal soupe with' through mid-[1086] 'seint Valenty'

object.description: Corresponding readings in Arch. Selden B.14; the selection spans are complete for this edition's use but physically partial relative to the manuscript

scope.notes: At Parson [104], Selden and Corpus change within a paragraph: Selden supplies through 'of which synne þe fame is openly' and Corpus resumes at 'spoken in þe contre'. At [290], Corpus runs through 'he shal soupe with' and Selden resumes within the same paragraph. E1646-1647 are printed from Selden despite no Corpus gap.

access.access_method: Corpus Christi College's Wright Special Collections Centre is closed to readers during relocation; ordinary appointments are unavailable until August 2027, while reprographic enquiries and digital-scan orders remain possible with delays.
notes: This is a dated temporary operational status, not a permanent denial; use the complete public Digital Bodleian facsimile when it meets the research need.

rights.component: Repository XML serialization and markup
rights.basis: No project-level license is inferred for corpus XML/data from any software license in the linked CME repository; the embedded source notice and current U-M statement conflict.

rights.component: Current U-M electronic item
rights.basis: The current U-M item designates the item Public Domain / No Copyright-United States; this is recorded as a provider assertion, not an independent worldwide conclusion.

rights.component: OCR and markup under the embedded 2006 availability notice
rights.basis: The XML header says copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply; written clarification is needed before high-risk standalone redistribution.

rights.component: Scanned page images
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies a No Copyright-United States designation, while no separate bulk-image terms were verified.

rights.component: Published edition text and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1868-1884, before January 1, 1931; Circular 15A supports public-domain status in the United States as of 2026.

rights.component: Physical manuscript object
rights.basis: Ownership and access control are distinct from copyright in a physical medieval object.

rights.component: Medieval text represented by the manuscript
rights.basis: The medieval text predates modern U.S. copyright; this conclusion does not cover modern photographs, catalog metadata, or uncertain treatment of historically unpublished passages outside the United States.

rights.component: Digital manuscript photographs
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian attributes the photographs to Corpus Christi College, permits non-commercial use with acknowledgment, and requires advance permission for commercial use including academic publication.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan object
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata records possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the 1868 carrier; this is a provider designation rather than a worldwide rights conclusion.

rights.component: Machine-generated OCR derivative
rights.basis: Underlying edition text is public domain in the United States, but no independent license or worldwide copyright conclusion for the OCR file was verified.

rights.component: Internet Archive metadata and scandata
rights.basis: Public API access does not by itself establish a reusable metadata license.

rights.component: Physical manuscript object
rights.basis: Physical ownership and reader access are separate from copyright in the medieval text or modern photographs.

rights.component: Selected-slide manuscript photographs
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian publishes the selected images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0.
notes: CC BY-NC 4.0 permits attributed noncommercial reuse. It does not authorize commercial print-on-demand or other commercial distribution; obtain separate permission for that use.

rights.component: Physical manuscript object
rights.basis: Physical ownership and access do not determine copyright in medieval text or modern reproductions.

rights.component: Modern digital manuscript images and IIIF presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The checked IIIF manifest supplies no explicit reuse license; public access does not itself establish an open license.
notes: Ask Huntington about uncertain reuse; this layer is separate from public-domain medieval content and Hooper's nineteenth-century engravings.

rights.component: Modern manuscript page images
rights.basis: Cambridge Digital Library publishes the manuscript images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0.
notes: Attributed noncommercial reuse is licensed; commercial print-on-demand is not licensed without separate permission. CUDL metadata has separate CC BY-NC-ND terms.

rights.component: 1775-1778 edition text and typography
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1775-1778.
notes: Modern scan files and platform metadata are separate layers.

rights.component: Two selected-slide manuscript photographs
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian's IIIF attribution publishes these images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0.
notes: Attributed noncommercial reuse is licensed; commercial print-on-demand requires separate permission.

rights.component: Twenty-three Ellesmere teller portrait designs as reproduced in Hooper's woodcuts for Furnivall's edition
rights.basis: Furnivall published woodcut reproductions of the twenty-three modeled portrait designs in 1868-1879; Furnivall and Hooper died in 1910 and 1912, so the historical published derivative layer's terms have expired.
notes: Limited to visual forms actually reproduced in the nineteenth-century woodcuts; not a license for Huntington's modern photographs.

rights.component: Original medieval pictorial designs, illumination, and decoration in the Ellesmere manuscript, excluding modern photography
rights.basis: The original artistic works were created with the manuscript around 1400-1410. Official UK guidance applies the ordinary artistic-work term and states that the special 2039 rule does not cover artistic works other than photographs and engravings; the original medieval artistic layer's term has expired.
notes: Concerns original medieval art, not Huntington's modern photographs, interface assets, or reproduction conditions.

rights.component: Original medieval artistic designs and decoration in Gg.4.27(1), excluding modern photography and metadata
rights.basis: The original drawings and decoration were created with the manuscript in the fifteenth century. Official UK guidance applies the ordinary artistic-work term and states that the special 2039 rule does not cover artistic works other than photographs and engravings; the original medieval artistic layer's term has expired.
notes: Concerns original medieval designs, not CUDL's modern photographs, interface, or metadata.

rights.component: Hooper's twenty-three woodcut derivatives of the Ellesmere teller portraits as issued with Furnivall's edition
rights.basis: The woodcuts were published in the nineteenth century; William Harcourt Hooper died in 1912 and the relevant term has expired.
notes: This historical derivative layer is separate from the medieval designs and modern Huntington photographs. The checked carrier retains fourteen and lacks nine of the announced twenty-three plates.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Corpus MS 198 Canterbury Tales and Gamelyn text, with identified lacunae and supplementary passages in other manuscripts
editorial_practices.representation: One-manuscript Corpus print with bracketed supplies, appendix texts, and visual apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's representation follows the Corpus order A, B-alpha, F, D, E, F, G, C, B-beta, H, I and makes many source switches visible. Corpus begins at A73 and ends after I290; supplied text is not silently assigned to Corpus MS 198.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire edition, especially bracketed passages, p. 698, and appendix pp. 1*-8*

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lacunae and supplementary passages from Arch. Selden B.14, Laud 600, Christ Church 152, Hatton donat.1, Harley 1758, Harley 1753, Royal 17 D XV, Harley 7333, Royal 19 C VII, and the unresolved Haistwell manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Brackets, notes, folio milestones, and separate appendices identify source roles at passage level
editorial_practices.description: Direct English supplies are modeled as transcriptions. Royal 19 C VII supplies only a cited Middle French phrase and is not an English base witness. Haistwell supplies only F679-680 within the otherwise Laud Squire-Franklin link.
editorial_practices.scope: Every explicit source milestone, bracket, and source note in AGZ8235.0001.001

editorial_practices.source_feature: Comparison notes for Laud Misc. 739, Barlow 20, Ellesmere, Royal 18 C II, Sloane 1685/1686, and generic Oxford manuscripts
editorial_practices.representation: Editorial comparison notes rather than direct source switches
editorial_practices.description: Laud Misc. 739 is cited for omission of Wife 716-720; Barlow 20 for omission of Wife 605-608; Ellesmere for Franklin 1493-1498; Royal 18 C II for a Nun's Priest omission; Sloane 1685/1686 for absence of the Harley 7333 extract; and other Oxford manuscripts generically. None is modeled as a direct ancestor of printed English text on this evidence alone.
editorial_practices.scope: Comparison-only notes across Wife, Franklin, and Nun's Priest passages

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed text, display matter, plates, and 756 scanned pages
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR with Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries structure and linked page images
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header states that no OCR correction or content editing was performed. Front/back matter and missing visual apparatus are represented through omission containers and page breaks rather than full transcription.

editorial_practices.representation: 756 unique PB references with contiguous SEQ 1-756
editorial_practices.description: The stable PB sequence supports exact page-image inventory claims but does not prove completeness of the intended multi-part edition's absent plate suites.

evidence.citation: General Corpus repository XML for AGZ8235.0001.001.
evidence.locator: HEADER; TEXT; source MILESTONE and NOTE1 contexts
evidence.summary: Fixes the U-M item, immediate source edition, witness order, exact content structure, uncorrected OCR policy, source switches, page-image references, and embedded rights notice.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME git object context for agz8235.xml.
evidence.summary: Fixes the repository source snapshot and git blob for the tracked artifact.

evidence.citation: Target-scoped structural and fixity audit of agz8235.xml.
evidence.summary: Confirms exact node counts, unique and contiguous page references, semantic verse counts excluding Gamelyn, and XML endpoints.

evidence.citation: Passage-level audit of every named manuscript source milestone, bracketed supply, and comparison note in agz8235.xml against the cached Furnivall PDF.
evidence.locator: MILESTONE UNIT values; NOTE1 source labels; main pp. 1-698 and appendix pp. 1*-8*
evidence.summary: Separates direct English transcription sources, the Royal 19 C VII French quotation, comparison-only witnesses, and the unresolved Haistwell label; establishes exact passage roles and locators.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English prose and verse, AGZ8235.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Current public text/page-image route, item metadata, table of contents, and Public Domain / No Copyright-United States designation.

evidence.citation: Deep structural, textual, pagination, and visual-apparatus audit of the Internet Archive PDF for corpusmscorpusch00chaurich.
evidence.locator: All 740 PDF pages; especially title/contents PDF 719-724, p. 698 at PDF 714, appendices PDF 725-732, and apparatus inventory PDF 733
evidence.summary: Confirms complete main text and textual appendices for the imperfect scanned carrier, exact printed order and page spans, source labels, seven-part issue context, combined 1868-1879 title, and the visual-apparatus inventory: fourteen of the announced twenty-three Ellesmere teller plates are recorded as surviving in the carrier and nine as missing, while the exact surviving subset and actual issuance of the separate Gg.4.27 suite remain unresolved.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API for corpusmscorpusch00chaurich.
evidence.summary: Fixes identifier, file inventory, bibliographic metadata, issue note, source library, exact file sizes, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT/US provider assertion.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scandata for corpusmscorpusch00chaurich.
evidence.summary: Machine-readable scan-leaf ordering and pagination metadata; contains no target text itself.

evidence.citation: Audit of the Internet Archive DjVu plain-text OCR against the cached PDF and XML.
evidence.locator: 1,419,183-byte plain text; headings and appendix transitions
evidence.summary: Supports discovery and page-transition checks without treating OCR as authoritative for readings.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust records 001640374 and 007517015 for Furnivall's Corpus-MS edition.
evidence.summary: Supports London 1868-1879 cataloging and the seven-part 1868-1884 issue note; not used to claim completeness of any individual digital volume.

evidence.summary: Internet Archive metadata establishes the original five-volume 1775-1778 edition; exact consulted tale ranges are documented by the separate volume 2 and volume 3 evidence records.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Ellesmere Chaucer, IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: Official 480-canvas presentation used to verify the twenty-three portrait loci and Franklin comparison loci.

evidence.citation: British Museum authority record for William Harcourt Hooper (1834-1912).
evidence.summary: Authority evidence for the wood engraver credited in the source edition with copying and cutting the twenty-three Ellesmere teller portraits.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, MS Gg.4.27.1, viewer and IIIF presentation.
evidence.summary: Official 1,144-canvas presentation used to verify the six teller and three paired allegorical image loci named in the edition's contents.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, catalogue description of Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27.
evidence.summary: Cambridge-hosted catalog evidence confirming the surviving figures and folio loci in Gg.4.27.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official term guidance supporting the distinction between exact nineteenth-century-published passages and residual manuscript content that may remain protected through 31 December 2039.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Catalogue of Western Medieval Manuscripts, MS. Barlow 20.
evidence.summary: Exact official item-level catalog linked by the provider's IIIF metadata; identifies the manuscript, third-quarter fifteenth-century date, England, parchment, and Canterbury Tales content.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, MS. Barlow 20 selected two-canvas presentation.
evidence.summary: Official selected surrogate contains exactly f. 5r and f. 44r and states CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms; the target D605-608 comparison locus is absent.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Archives & Manuscripts Requests.
evidence.summary: Official reading-room request route for manuscripts listed online or in printed catalogs, including template requests by exact shelfmark.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, Order Images.
evidence.summary: Official paid order and reproduction route for special-collections images unavailable through Digital Bodleian, requiring exact shelfmark and folio/page.

evidence.citation: British Library, Reading Rooms and collection-item requests.
evidence.summary: Official access route: a valid Reader Pass permits advance request of manuscript collection items for consultation.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Official paid digitisation and reproduction route requiring exact shelfmark, title, and folio/page numbers.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Using the Library and Aeon services.
evidence.summary: Official routes for research access, virtual reading-room appointments, and reproduction orders.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Official imaging and permissions request route for private-study, academic, and commercial uses.

evidence.summary: Complete original-volume scan containing the exact Parson's Tale paragraphing range Furnivall says he retained; file size, SHA-256, page count, PDF structure, and boundary headings were checked.

evidence.summary: Verified provider manifest, exact two-image member set, inventory source URLs, sizes, SHA-256 values, ZIP CRCs, and decodable JPEGs. The selection is provider-complete but physically partial and lacks D605-608.

evidence.citation: Corpus Christi College Oxford, Visitors to the Special and Historic Collections.
evidence.summary: Official current notice: the special-collections center is closed during relocation, ordinary reader appointments are unavailable, and access remains extremely limited until August 2027; contact Archives by exact shelfmark.

evidence.citation: Corpus Christi College Oxford, Digitisation and Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Official route for digital scans, professional photography, microfilm-derived manuscript scans, and publication permission; relocation is causing delays until August 2027.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, Special Collections reader information.
evidence.summary: Official institution-specific route: external researchers may consult special collections by advance appointment and exact catalog reference; MS 152 is listed in the Western Manuscripts collection.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, Imaging Services and Photography.
evidence.summary: Official image-supply and reproduction-permission route, including contact, prices, and permission requirements; new high-resolution photography may be limited, but the Library invites exact-item enquiries.

open_questions.question: Which exact Oxford manuscripts lie behind Furnivall's generic statements that a Wife of Bath supply occurs in 'no other Oxford MS.'?
next_steps: Locate Furnivall's manuscript sigla table or working papers that identify the compared Oxford set.
Check later Six-Text apparatus scholarship for a source-backed shelfmark crosswalk.
Do not mint witness entities until an exact source identifies them.

open_questions.question: Does Furnivall's historical 'Sloane 1686, f. 285' correspond exactly to the current British Library Sloane MS 1686 item?
next_steps: Request or inspect an image of current Sloane MS 1686 f. 285.
Compare the Nun's Priest context around B4233-4237 against Furnivall's omission note.
Retain medium confidence until shelfmark and locus are confirmed together.

open_questions.question: Which fourteen of the twenty-three Ellesmere teller plates survive in the checked carrier, and were the nine announced Gg.4.27 woodcuts ever issued?
next_steps: Inspect a physically complete institutional carrier or original issue wrappers for the exact plate inventory.
Reconcile the Internet Archive scandata's excluded images against the physical carrier.
Record the surviving Ellesmere subset and any Gg suite only when directly visible or cataloged.
